report_title: Domain Shadowing: A Stealthy Use of DNS Compromise for Cybercrime

iocs.context: Compromised parent domain under which attackers created malicious shadowed subdomains as part of a phishing campaign; domain owners later discovered the compromise.
iocs.source_section: Table 1 / A Phishing Campaign Using Shadowed Domains

iocs.context: Shadowed subdomain under compromised halont.edu.au with IP address in Russia (62.204.41.218), active for less than 1 month; attacker accidentally hijacked it via DNS wildcarding.
Malicious shadowed subdomain under compromised halont.edu.au with Russian IP address 62.204.41.218, active for less than 1 day.
Malicious shadowed subdomain under compromised halont.edu.au with Russian IP address 62.204.41.218, active for less than 1 day.
Compromised parent domain (~19 years old) under which attackers created many malicious subdomains used in phishing links to harvest credentials.
iocs.source_section: Table 1 / A Phishing Campaign Using Shadowed Domains

iocs.context: Shadowed subdomain under compromised barwonbluff.com.au with Russian IP address 62.204.41.247, active for approximately 3 months as part of a phishing campaign.
Shadowed subdomain used in phishing links redirecting victims to a fake Microsoft login page on elitepackagingblog.com to harvest credentials.
iocs.source_section: A Phishing Campaign Using Shadowed Domains

iocs.context: Malicious shadowed subdomain under compromised barwonbluff.com.au with Russian IP address 62.204.41.77, active for less than 1 day.
Compromised parent domain (~12 years old) under which attackers created malicious shadowed subdomains leveraging its benign reputation for phishing.
Shadowed subdomain under compromised brisbanegateway.com with Russian IP address 62.204.41.218, active for approximately 2 days as part of a phishing campaign.
Compromised parent domain (~17 years old) under which attackers created malicious shadowed subdomains as part of the phishing campaign.
Malicious shadowed subdomain under compromised vembanadhouse.com with Russian IP address 62.204.41.218, active for less than 1 day.
Phishing landing page that harvests Microsoft user credentials; victims are redirected here from shadowed domains such as snaitechbumxzzwt.barwonbluff.com.au.
iocs.source_section: A Phishing Campaign Using Shadowed Domains
